001/**
002 * Application entry point for the Zuul-style Pokémon adventure game.
003 * <p>
004 * This class wires the {@link GameEngine} to the {@link UserInterface} so that
005 * player input is displayed and processed through a single {@code main}-less
006 * bootstrap (typical BlueJ style: instantiate {@code Game} to start).
007 * </p>
008 *
009 * @author Barnabe Jouanard
010 * @version 2026.02.17
011 */
012public class Game {
013    /** Swing-based view that shows text, images, and command controls. */
014    private UserInterface aGui;
015    /** Core game logic: rooms, parser, and command interpretation. */
016    private GameEngine aEngine;
017    /** Asset manager responsible for downloading missing files at startup. */
018    private AssetManager aAssetManager;
019
020    /**
021     * Constructs the game by creating the engine and GUI, then linking them.
022     * <p>
023     * The GUI receives a reference to the engine; the engine is given the GUI
024     * so it can print messages and update the display after construction.
025     * </p>
026     */
027    public Game() {
028        this.aAssetManager = new AssetManager();
029        if (!aAssetManager.allAssetsCached()) {
030            LoadingScreen vLoading = new LoadingScreen(aAssetManager);
031            vLoading.startAndWait();
032        }
033
034        this.aEngine = new GameEngine();
035        this.aGui = new UserInterface(this.aEngine);
036        this.aEngine.setGUI(this.aGui);
037    } // Game()
038} // Game
